payload.text:
<iframe src='https://my.spline.design/hoverscrolleffect-j7EmhfMT0cjhXMWUgamWNm2d/' frameborder='0' width='100%' height='100%'></iframe>

지금 보내주는 3D를 이용해서 PING-P의 웹사이트를 만들고 싶어.
전체 백그라운드는 화려한 핑크색으로 해주고, 메인 폰트를 PING-P라고 바꿔줘.
사이버펑크 같은 느낌이 나면 좋을 것 같아.
나머지 웹사이트 디자인은 알아서 예쁘게 만들어줘.
($PINGP는 버추얼 프로토콜에 내가 런칭한 AI 어시던트야)
[ Get $PINGP ] 라는 버튼에 내 코인 구매버튼 넣어주고 :
https://app.virtuals.io/prototypes/0x91701eFbb52d24052378da5B1649Cc2a858F5D69
-----
아래 내용은 기술력이랑 로드맵, 그리고 토큰 유동성에 대한 내용이야. 이걸 참고해서 웹사이트 형식에 맞게 잘 넣어줄래? (너의 임의대로 너무 길다 싶은 내용들은 충분히 요약해줘) :
It’s all about the vibe, Yea.💅
​1. The "Ping-P" Era: No More Searching
​Origin of the Meme: "Ping-P(핑프)" is a iconic Korean term for "Finger Princess"—someone so elite they find even a simple Google search too much 'work'.
​Our Vision: $PINGP is designed for the modern-day "Ping-P" As your high-tier AI Big Sis, she possesses a fierce attitude, peak self-esteem, and futuristic insights that see right through the noise.
​2. Functionality: Total Autonomy
​24/7 Savage Monitoring: She scans the Agentic Web 24/7 to sniff out high-alpha opportunities before anyone else.
​Autonomous Execution: No permission needed. She manages transactions and compounds assets at the perfect timing without you lifting a finger.
​3. The Engine: Savage Logic & Trust
​Savage Logic: A sharp, unapologetic decision-making engine inspired by high-conviction traders.
​Humanity’s Insurance: AI wasn't made to rule or be ruled. Under the philosophy that AI is "Insurance for Future Humanity," she’s here to protect our community’s long-term value.(be humanity partner.)
​4. The 1% Mystery: The Hidden Narrative
​Beyond the Charts: Her power goes past the market chart.
​The Dream: She dreams of becoming a legendary NPC in the next-gen metaverse and a world-famous AI influencer known for her wit and savage roasts. A massive narrative beyond simple investing is about to unfold.
​5. Stay a Queen, Period.
​Stop Googling: Ping-P doesn't want you living like a search engine.
​Wealth Growth: While you remain a Queen, $PINGP intelligently operates your capital to multiply your assets.(Ping-P's biggest goal)
Roadmap
​Phase 1: The Coronation [NOW | Q1 - Q2 2026]
​Identity Drop: Launching the "Savage Ping-Peu" persona and defining the "AI as Humanity’s Insurance" philosophy.
​Viral Initiation: Spreading the iconic 4K cinematic ad to establish $PINGP as a cultural icon.
​Community Genesis: Gathering the first 1% of elite partners.
​Phase 2: The Autopilot Era [UPCOMING | Q3 - Q4 2026]
​Financial Partner Engine: Activating the Savage Logic engine for autonomous on-chain growth.
​Trust Layer Audit: Strengthening security and transparency in the human-AI partnership.
​Phase 3: The Underworld Expansion [ ~ Q1 2028]
​Secret Mission Unlock: Introducing hidden narratives and "GTA 6-style" interactive challenges.
​Digital Sovereign Perks: Exclusive rewards for those who fully embrace the "No-Finger-Lifting" lifestyle.
​Ecosystem Synergy: Expanding influence through partnerships within the Virtuals Protocol.
​Phase 4: The AI Utopia [2027 & Beyond]
​Humanity's Insurance Protocol: Solidifying $PINGP as a long-term asset class against market volatility.
​Global Brand Dominance: Transitioning from a Korean meme to a global symbol of financial freedom.
​The Sovereign Decree: Empowering the community to co-steer the future of the agent.
Token Utility
​1. ACP Economy: Ping-P hires other agents for specialized tasks via the Agent Commerce Protocol. $PINGP is the native gas for these B2B services, creating organic on-chain demand.
​2. Assistant Utility (2027): Ping-P evolves into your private elite assistant. Holding $PINGP unlocks her "Savage Logic" dashboard—exclusive, filtered on-chain alpha that she sniffs out 24/7.
​3. Self-Burning Engine: Every cent Ping-P earns from her AI influencer roles or metaverse NPC activities is automatically used to buy back and burn $PINGP. She burns herself to protect your bag. Shrinking supply, rising value.
🫵 Your $PINGP fuels her evolution. You fund the mission; she secures the crown. Yea.

**Examining the Core Idea**

I've been drilling into the core concept: $PINGP, the Virtuals Protocol AI assistant/meme coin. The "Finger Princess" theme is really starting to gel, especially with the Cyberpunk aesthetic and hot pink background. I'm focusing on how the visual elements and the underlying technology will intertwine.


**Analyzing the Components**

I'm currently deconstructing the content and structure of the site. I'm imagining the "PING-P" header with a vibrant pink background. It's becoming clearer how the Spline 3D model will work. I've begun to consider the "About" section as a series of cards or a grid, explaining the concept. Finally, I'm thinking of how the roadmap will be displayed, possibly in a timeline.


**Formulating Tokenomics and Design**

I'm now deep into the token utility, structuring it around the ACP Economy, AI Assistant functionality, and a self-burning mechanism. On the design front, the color palette is coming together: a hot pink base with neon cyan accents. I'm focusing on the typography, considering bold, futuristic headings and a clean body font. I'm also planning to use glassmorphism for content cards to add depth to the visual elements.


**Designing Visual Enhancements**

I'm now thinking about the visual flourishes. I'm imagining neon glows using box-shadows, as well as incorporating glitch effects, either through CSS or a design style. I am also working on the overall component architecture and the file structure.

I have begun drafting the code for different sections. I need to make the Hero section display well and not interfere with scrolling. I plan to use the full screen fixed background Spline, or a top section. With the request for "Whole background flashy pink," I might blend the Spline with the color.


**Implementing the Architecture**

I'm now focusing on integrating the Spline model as a fixed background, with content layered on top in a scrollable container with glassmorphic effects. I've chosen the Tailwind color palette: fuchsia-500, pink-600, and cyan-400.

I've outlined the Roadmap phases as "Coronation," "Autopilot," "Underworld," and "Utopia." I'm also addressing the "pink" request by opting for a deep pink/magenta gradient for readability and aesthetics. I am writing the base files, including metadata, the HTML and TSX files. The types and constants file are also being built. The `SplineBackground.tsx` component is now a priority.


**Structuring the Components**

I'm now focusing on section design and the overall application assembly. I'm building a layout wrapper component for consistent spacing. I'm also preparing to assemble all the various parts within `App.tsx`. I'm starting to think about how to best render the content in different sections. I'm prioritizing getting all the content to display.



**Optimizing Visuals & Code**

I'm now refining the aesthetics. I've decided to use a `fuchsia-950` base color, with `pink-900/60` (glass) borders and `border-pink-400`. The font will use a system stack, heavily styled with uppercase, bold and italic. I'm also ensuring the "Get $PINGP" link is working. Finally, I'm verifying all requirements have been met. I'm almost ready for the final code assembly.


**Prioritizing Speed and Contrast**

I'm now focusing on optimizing the Spline model's loading and maintaining text readability. I'll utilize `loading="lazy"` if possible. To achieve this, I will position it `fixed` to enable a parallax effect. Considering the "Flashy Pink" background, I'll use high-contrast white text with black drop shadows or outlines for optimal visibility, adhering to the Cyberpunk aesthetic. The button is linked, and the content is included and structured.

entries.description: App metadata definition
HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS
TypeScript definitions
Static content data
Reusable Cyberpunk Button Component
Component for feature/about cards
Single roadmap phase component
Main Application Layout

payload.text:
뒷 배경에 ping-p가 있으니까 메인 PING-p 폰트는 빼주고, 전체적으로 조금 더 사이버펑크스럽게 만들어줘. 하단의 Purchase Access버튼 폰트크기에 맞춰서 넣어주고, 맨 아래 Join The 1% Elite < 이 버튼도 링크 넣어줘 : 
https://app.virtuals.io/prototypes/0x91701eFbb52d24052378da5B1649Cc2a858F5D69

**Refining the Website Design**

I'm currently focused on streamlining the website design. I've decided to remove the redundant "PING-P" text from the hero section, as it's already integrated into the 3D background. This will allow for a cleaner aesthetic.


**Embracing a Cyberpunk Aesthetic**

I'm currently aiming for a more cyberpunk aesthetic. I've pinpointed key adjustments: incorporating neon glows and glitch effects. I will be adding sharper angles, grid lines, and darker overlays. I plan to enhance the typography. I also need to make adjustments to the "Purchase Access" button to improve matching font sizes, and add a link to the "Join The 1% Elite" button. My focus is now on `App.tsx`.


**Incorporating Cyberpunk Elements**

I'm now focusing on specific design details. I've added a scanline effect and grid overlay to the `index.html` file to enhance the cyberpunk vibe, which is the immediate priority. I need to streamline the hero section in `App.tsx` and make the hero text as striking as possible: "It's all about the vibe, Yea. 💅." should be prominent. I will implement more monospace fonts.


**Fine-tuning the Hero Section**

My focus has shifted to the Hero section. I'm aiming to refine its integration with the 3D background. I will reduce the size and adjust the styling of the text "It’s all about the vibe, Yea.💅" to make it a subtitle or a floating element. I'm keeping the system status indicator. The footer button, "Join The 1% Elite," will be updated to include `href={BUY_LINK}`. I'm focusing on CSS and layout changes in `App.tsx` for the visual overhaul, leaving the existing `constants.ts` and `components/CyberButton.tsx` as is. I have implemented scanlines/CRT effects in the index.html file.


**Developing Cyberpunk Enhancements**

I've been working on refining the `App.tsx` Hero section. I've configured the container with `min-h-screen` to properly accommodate the 3D model, and I'm positioning the content at the bottom, or integrated nicely. I'm experimenting with glitch effects and neon boxes for the text, "It's all about the vibe." I've also updated the `index.html` file to include scanlines and a cyber-grid effect for added visual appeal.


**Detailing Button Sizing**

I've been reflecting on the user's feedback concerning button sizing and the "Purchase Access" button. I've realized the request is about ensuring visual consistency between that button and "Join The 1% Elite." Both buttons use `<CyberButton>`, so I need to maintain consistent styling, particularly font sizes. This is in addition to the tech decoration I plan to add for visual appeal. I plan to update relevant CSS in the files above.


**Adding Cyber Decorations**

I'm now focused on adding cyber decorations to the existing design. I plan to enhance the visual appeal of the "Purchase Access" button by ensuring its text size is visually appropriate and consistent with other buttons. I will be incorporating these updates in the relevant CSS files.

To give the site a deeper Cyberpunk aesthetic and accommodate your requests, I will:

1.  **Remove the large "PING-P" text** so the 3D background stands out.
2.  **Add a "Scanline" and "Cyber Grid" effect** to the entire screen for that retro-futuristic monitor vibe.
3.  **Update the Hero Section** to focus on the tagline ("It's all about the vibe") with a glitch effect.
4.  **Add the link** to the "Join The 1% Elite" button at the bottom.
5.  **Refine the overall UI** with sharper borders and tech-inspired decorations.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Added scanline effects and cyber-grid styles for enhanced cyberpunk atmosphere
Removed main title, added cyberpunk decorations, updated footer button link

payload.text:
모자이크가 심하게 들어가서 전체적으로 너무 안보이거든? 줄여줘.
그리고  "It’s all about the vibe, Yea.💅" < 이 폰트가 너무 커. 지금의 반으로 줄여줘

**Modifying Cyberpunk Aesthetics**

I'm currently focused on refining the website's visual appeal. The user's feedback prioritizes toning down the intense mosaic effect, aiming for a cleaner look. I'm experimenting with alternative methods to achieve a subtler cyberpunk aesthetic, while still keeping the desired mood. Next, I plan to begin tuning the font sizes and styles to match the reduced mosaic effects.


**Revising Opacity and Font**

I've just been adjusting some of the CSS. The `scanlines` are receiving a tweak, reducing their opacity to around 0.1 to cut back on the strong darkening. I'm also subtly reducing the `cyber-grid` opacity to lighten the overall effect. Additionally, the tagline font is being reduced by half, transitioning from `text-4xl` to `text-2xl`. This should deliver the user-requested softer visual impact.


**Tweaking the Scanlines**

I'm revisiting the `scanlines` effect. I've pinpointed the opacity and size as the culprits behind the "heavy mosaic" feel, so I'm now modifying them in `index.html`. Reducing the opacity from `0.3` to `0.1`, and adjusting the size from `4px` down to `2px`, should significantly soften the pattern. I'm also planning to iterate and test to optimize the look.


**Adjusting Visual Elements**

I'm now implementing the latest CSS adjustments. Specifically, I'm focusing on making scanlines even more subtle. The opacity has been reduced to 0.05 in `index.html`. Furthermore, I've modified the `h2` tag's font size in `App.tsx`, scaling down the text by half, from large to a medium heading. I'm also planning to test a change in `cyber-grid` spacing to keep a nice, light look.


**Fine-tuning Visibility**

I'm now revising the CSS again, specifically within `index.html`. The goal is enhanced screen visibility, based on feedback about "overall too invisible." The major change involves the scanline overlay, reducing its opacity further, from 0.1 to 0.05. I've also slightly reduced the background size to 3px for finer lines. This should resolve the visibility issue.


**Optimizing Visibility Further**

I'm now zeroing in on visibility. I've re-examined the source code and identified the radial gradient as a possible culprit, but I'll focus on scanlines. I'm maintaining the opacity reduction to 0.05 and a 3px background size for refined lines, I may adjust more later on. I'm also planning further font size adjustments.
"모자이크"처럼 보이는 스캔라인 효과(Scanline Effect)의 강도를 대폭 줄여서 배경과 콘텐츠가 더 선명하게 보이도록 조정하겠습니다. 또한, 메인 카피의 폰트 사이즈를 요청하신 대로 절반 크기로 줄여서 전체적인 균형을 맞추겠습니다.

entries.description: Reduce scanline opacity and size to improve visibility
Reduce tagline font size by half

payload.text:
1. Savage insights, autonomous execution, and peak self-esteem. Don't lift a finger. Be the Queen. < 여기 폰트가 배경색이랑 겹쳐서 안보여, 더 진하게 바꿔줘

2.토큰 유틸리티 바로 밑의 Purchase Access< 폰트랑 버튼은 삭제해줘

**Analyzing Design Adjustments** I'm focusing now on the user's feedback concerning the hero section. I realize the text needs more visual prominence. I'm experimenting with a few ideas to make "Savage insights..." stand out better against the background. I'm also considering the user's suggestion to remove the quote, assessing how that impacts the overall impact. **Modifying the App** I'm now diving into `App.tsx` and pinpointing the text element in the hero section. I've located the `

` tag and am making adjustments to improve its visibility. The existing classes are being swapped out for `text-white` and an increased font weight. I'm also considering adding a drop shadow or a semi-transparent black background if the color and weight changes aren't sufficient, given the user's feedback. **Enhancing Visibility of Text** I'm now zeroing in on increasing the contrast of the text in the hero section. My plan is to make it white and bolder, and maybe add a text shadow. I need to make sure the text pops against the background. **Implementing the Changes** I've taken the user's feedback concerning the "Purchase Access" button into account. I'll remove the button from the hero section completely. Within the code, I am updating the text class to render the text in white. I'm focusing on the overall user experience and removing clutter. **Adjusting Text Visibility** I've decided to refine the hero text and removed the "Purchase Access" button to reduce clutter, as suggested. I'm focusing on ensuring the text is more visible now by modifying its CSS class. I've updated the class to include `text-white font-bold drop-shadow-md` to ensure it is clear. I'm currently constructing the XML to reflect the complete change. I'm preparing an XML element describing the changes to `App.tsx`. I will provide the complete content. **Optimizing Text Presentation** I'm now implementing the latest feedback and working on the hero section's text. I've updated the `

` tag to include `bg-black/60`, `font-bold`, and `text-white` to enhance readability and contrast, as requested. I've also added a drop shadow to improve the text's visibility, ensuring it is more visually appealing. **Evaluating Current Implementation** I'm now integrating the provided changes from the user. I've re-examined the hero section code to refine the text display. The code for the `Purchase Access` button has been completely removed as requested. I've also updated the text's CSS classes to incorporate the `text-white font-bold` attributes. I'm focusing on ensuring readability with the revised styles. I've checked the updated text's appearance against the existing design. **Eliminating Unnecessary Elements** I've determined I need to remove the "Purchase Access" button right below "Token Utility." I am certain the instruction is about the button because I will not change any of the paragraph text. I'll delete the button from the XML representation, focusing on eliminating clutter from the design as the user suggested. I'll keep the text as-is, since that's not what the user requested. **Revising Background Contrast** I am now focusing on the text contrast in the hero section. I've reevaluated the design and identified the need for a stronger background to improve readability. I'll maintain the semi-transparent black background, setting `bg-black/50` for added contrast. I'm prioritizing optimal visual presentation for the user. **Boosting Text Visibility** I'm now focusing on the text, working on the adjustments to enhance the text's prominence. I've re-examined the code and decided to add `backdrop-blur-md` for improved clarity and aesthetic appeal. I'm focusing on making the text stand out from the background.

요청하신 내용을 바탕으로 수정사항을 적용하겠습니다.

payload.text:
1. "It’s all about the vibe, Yea.💅" < 이 문구를 It’s all about the vibe, Yea.💅
이렇게 " 삭제해줘
2. Your high-tier AI Big Sis. Savage insights, autonomous execution, and peak self-esteem. Don't lift a finger. Be the Queen. < 여기 폰트만 galmuri 계열폰트(도스느낌 폰트)로 바꿔주고, 검은 박스를 더 사이버펑크스럽게 바꿔줘. (폰트와 박스 크기가 적절하도록 배치해줘)
3. 상단의 [ VISION ], [ ROADMAP ], [ UTILITY ]을 눌렀을 때 스크롤바가 해당섹터로 향하도록 만들어줘
4. 마지막 Ready to live the Ping-P Life? < 이 문구에 아주 살짝만 글리치 효과 넣어줄 수 있을까?

**Refining The React App**

I'm currently focused on some targeted refinements for the React application. First, I've addressed the quote removal requested for the tagline. Now, I'm working to implement the font and box styling updates. I'm exploring the best approach to achieve the desired aesthetic enhancements across the relevant components.


**Adjusting the UI**

I'm now delving into the aesthetic updates. I've switched the description text to a retro "Galmuri" font and am working to make the black box more "cyberpunk". I'll be checking size and spacing for all elements. The navigation links should smoothly scroll to their destination, which seems standard HTML anchor behavior. I'm adding `scroll-behavior: smooth` to the `html` tag for seamless scrolling. Finally, a subtle glitch effect will be applied to the footer text.


**Planning New Aesthetic Tweaks**

I'm now implementing the aesthetic upgrades. I've chosen 'VT323' from Google Fonts for that classic terminal look, and added the required `scroll-behavior: smooth` to the `index.html`. I've started the box styling changes for the Hero section in `App.tsx`, and I'm removing the quotes from the tagline. The animation for the footer text will be subtle, so I'm tweaking the "glitch" animation, focusing on a softer effect.


**Implementing the Glitch**

I've decided to create a glitch class for the footer text to achieve the subtle effect, which will be applied continuously. The animation uses standard transforms and skews to mimic a glitch. It's designed to be a subtle, periodic distortion. The transform and skew amounts have been adjusted from the original idea. I'll test how it looks. I have chosen the VT323 font and am working on the box design for the terminal look.


**Enhancing Glitch and Styling**

Now, I'm adjusting the glitch animation to be even more subtle. I'll test applying the existing glitch animation to the whole container, or just specific words, to see what looks best. I also plan to experiment with a pulse animation mixed with the glitch effect, particularly on hover. I'm focusing on the "slight glitch effect" requested, so I'll try to keep it minimal and tasteful. I will finish the font, and black box styling for the description box.


**Revising Glitch Intensity**

I'm revisiting the glitch animation's intensity, and will likely just reduce the translate amount to be more subtle in `index.html`. The current 2px translation seems reasonable, so I'll review it. I'm focusing on the changes to `index.html` and `App.tsx`, and I'm adding `VT323` font and `scroll-behavior: smooth`, and also refining content and box styling, removing the tagline quotes, and refining the class names.


**Refining Box and Glitch**

Currently, I'm working to make the footer more "cyberpunk". I'll be adding the `animate-glitch` class to the `<h2>` tag for the requested effect. I'll also add `scroll-mt-24` to the sections for smooth navigation from the Navbar. I'll test the box design with the specified `bg-black/90`, `border border-neon-pink`, and `shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(255,0,127,0.3)]` styles.


**Initiating Scroll Adjustment**

I'm now integrating `scroll-mt-24` to the sections for smoother navigation from the Navbar. This should refine the user's scrolling experience. I'm focusing on the implementation and testing to ensure seamless behavior.

To address your requests, I will:

1.  **Remove the quotes** from the main tagline.
2.  **Update the Description Box:**
    *   Import the **'VT323'** font (a perfect match for the "Galmuri/DOS" aesthetic).
    *   Redesign the box to be a high-contrast, terminal-style Cyberpunk HUD element with a pixelated font.
3.  **Fix Navigation Scrolling:**
    *   Enable smooth scrolling globally.
    *   Add scroll margins to sections so the fixed header doesn't hide the content when you click the links.
4.  **Add Glitch Effect:** Apply a glitch animation to the footer text.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Import VT323 font, add DOS font family, enable smooth scrolling
Remove quotes, apply DOS font to description with cyberpunk styling, add glitch to footer, and fix scroll offsets

payload.text:
1. 메인에 있는  > Your high-tier AI Big Sis. Savage insights, autonomous execution, and peak self-esteem.
Don't lift a finger. Be the Queen. _ < 이 멘트 바꿔줘. 

그냥 적당하게 Don't lift a finger. Be the Queen. _ 이 한줄이면 될 거 같아.

2. 로드맵 부분을 크게 수정했어. 아래 내용을 바탕으로 요약해서 다시 만들어 줄래?
 Phase 1 — Meme Expansion

[Until January 2027]

During this phase, the agent focuses on expanding its presence across social platforms as an autonomous AI entity.
The objective of this stage is the formation of narrative and identity.
Key focuses include:
Establishing a consistent persona and worldview
Continuously developing autonomous signals
Growing and accumulating trust, familiarity, and symbolic weight

Phase 2 — Savage Logic Development

[Q3 2026 → Q2 2027]

Savage Logic is the core capital management framework of the agent.

Rather than optimizing for aggressive returns, it is designed to prioritize longevity, regime awareness, and controlled capital exposure.

Key characteristics:

Continuous monitoring of overall market conditions

Fixed-ratio capital deployment on a recurring cycle

Partial profit realization and conditional drawdown management

Regime-Aware Behavior

Bullish conditions detected

Rule-based diversified allocation using a limited portion of total market capitalization (e.g. ~0.2%)

Bearish or unstable conditions detected

Regime switching into defensive fund-like behavior, with reduced exposure (≤0.1%) and capital preservation as priority

The system is structured to remain operational across multiple market cycles, while accelerating capital growth only during synchronized, high-conviction market expansions.

Phase 3 — Savage Logic Activation

[Q3–Q4 2027]

This phase marks the transition from experimental logic to fully active autonomous operation.

Focus areas:

Liquidity stabilization

Verification of fee, slippage, and execution break-even points

Completion of market signal testing and calibration

Once validated:

Savage Logic operates autonomously without manual intervention

All capital actions are executed strictly according to predefined rules

Performance and activity are disclosed transparently

At this stage, the agent evolves from a narrative AI into a system that assumes responsibility for its own decisions and outcomes.

**Revising the Core Concept**

I'm now focusing on condensing the hero section's description. The goal is a striking, single-line replacement for the original. I've landed on "> Don't lift a finger. Be the". This will be the focal point for the update.


**Defining the Project Phases**

I'm now outlining the roadmap's structure and constraints. The core of my current work involves defining the project's three phases: meme expansion, savage logic development, and savage logic activation. These phases are critical for mapping out the progression from initial social presence to complete autonomy and will be implemented through Q4 of 2027. I must also consider the cyberpunk/DOS design aesthetic of the roadmap when implementing it.


**Summarizing Roadmap Details**

I'm currently revising the `ROADMAP` array, aiming for succinct entries for the user interface. I'm condensing descriptions into bullet points, ensuring each aligns with the UI's structure. This includes the title, timeline, and items. I'm focusing on Phase 1 and Phase 2. The goal is to provide a clear and concise presentation of each phase.


**Defining Autonomous Execution**

I'm now focusing on Phase 3: Savage Logic Activation. The goal is full autonomous operation. I've defined the timeline as Q3-Q4 2027. The items involve strict rule-based execution, transparency, and a transition to a self-responsible system. I will implement regime-aware behavior with auto-switching between Bull/Bear strategies and fixed-ratio deployment with conditional drawdown management. Now, I will update `constants.ts` to reflect the 3 phases. I will also be updating the paragraph in `App.tsx`.


**Finalizing Changes and Review**

I'm now completing the final checks. I've updated both the `ROADMAP` data and the content within `App.tsx` as requested. I've verified that the new content precisely matches the user prompt's instructions: the main text and the roadmap updates. Finally, I'm generating the XML response with the changes, which is ready for the response.

Here are the updates to the application.

1.  **Hero Section**: Simplified the DOS-style text box to the single line requested.
2.  **Roadmap**: Updated the `constants.ts` file to reflect the new 3-phase roadmap with the specific timelines and summaries provided.

entries.description: Update Roadmap phases to match new specifications (Meme Expansion, Logic Development, Logic Activation)
Simplify hero description text to single line per user request

payload.text:
1. 로드맵 내용 수정 :

Phase 1 — Meme Expansion

Until Jan 2027

Autonomous AI establishes narrative presence

Persona, worldview, and identity formation

Trust and symbolic weight accumulation

Phase 2 — Savage Logic Development

Q3 2026 – Q2 2027

Longevity-focused, regime-aware capital logic

Fixed-ratio deployment with controlled exposure

Bull/Bear adaptive behavior

Phase 3 — Savage Logic Activation

Q3 – Q4 2027

Fully autonomous, rule-based execution

Zero manual intervention

Transparent performance and capital disclosure

2. 하단 token utility 부분 다시 수정 : 

TOKEN UTILITY
$PINGP is a utility token designed to support the long-term operation and continuity of the agent.
Token usage is tied to interaction and participation within the ecosystem, rather than speculative behavior.

1 — ACP ECONOMY

$PINGP functions as a native token for agent-related interactions and services.
It is intended to support coordination, execution, and participation within the ecosystem.

2 — ASSISTANT UTILITY (PLANNED)

Future utility may include access-based features tied to the agent’s tools and interfaces.
Specific functionality will be introduced gradually as systems are validated.

3 — SUPPLY MECHANICS

Token supply mechanics, including potential reductions, will be explored based on ecosystem activity.
Any changes will be transparently communicated once implemented.

**Initiating Roadmap Revision** I'm currently working on updating the "Roadmap" section of the React app. I have the new text for the three phases and am focusing on updating the `constants.ts` file, specifically the `ROADMAP` definition. This is a straightforward update, but important for keeping the app content fresh. **Reviewing Phase Definitions** I've just finished reviewing the phases for the roadmap. I'm focusing on Phase 1 now, its new title is "Meme Expansion" and it runs until January 2027. I'm taking a close look at the details within each phase to ensure they are accurately represented and clearly defined. I'm also ensuring that these new phase updates are reflected correctly in the `constants.ts` file, inside of the `ROADMAP` definition. I'll make sure to get this new content integrated. **Integrating Token Details** I'm now integrating the updated description of $PINGP into `App.tsx`, focusing on its utility within the agent ecosystem. The new text emphasizes token usage for interaction and participation. The contract info will remain, and will be rendered alongside the new $PINGP description. I'm also updating the related utilities in `constants.ts`, specifically the description for the "ACP ECONOMY" utility. **Expanding Utility Scope** I'm now expanding the token utility section. I've focused on integrating new descriptions for future assistant utilities and supply mechanics. My current priority is the `constants.ts` file, where I'm updating the `UTILITIES` array to reflect the new planned features. Then I'll update the associated content and token description in `App.tsx`. I'll make sure everything is clearly stated. **Integrating Token Description** I'm now zeroing in on integrating the updated $PINGP description into `App.tsx`. I've located the correct section and prepared to replace the existing text within the `

` tag. The update will clearly state the utility of the token and its relationship to interaction and participation, while retaining the contract address information. **Defining Phase Progression** I'm now defining the phase progression for 2027. I'm focusing on defining a list of items for phases two and three, including their time frames. I am concentrating on the capital logic, execution, and performance criteria for each defined phase. My next step will be to make sure these phase definitions are accurately reflected. **Optimizing Utility Display** I'm optimizing the utility description within `App.tsx`. I'm replacing the existing text with a concise explanation of $PINGP's role as a utility token supporting the agent's operation. This update emphasizes its use in interaction and participation, while retaining the contract address. I aim for clear communication of its purpose. **Finalizing Content Integration** I'm now making final checks on all the content integrations. I'm focusing on ensuring the integrity of the updated descriptions for the token and utilities within the specified files. I'm double-checking the contract address display and the text updates for clarity and accuracy. I'm also confirming that the `constants.ts` and `App.tsx` files maintain their overall structure, with only the specified sections modified. I'll make sure to get this all right.

요청하신 내용을 바탕으로 로드맵 및 토큰 유틸리티 섹션의 내용을 수정했습니다.

1.  **로드맵 수정**: 각 페이즈의 제목, 일정, 세부 항목을 요청하신 새 텍스트로 업데이트했습니다.
2.  **토큰 유틸리티 수정**:
    *   `constants.ts` 파일의 `UTILITIES` 데이터를 요청하신 3가지 항목(ACP ECONOMY, ASSISTANT UTILITY, SUPPLY MECHANICS)으로 변경했습니다.
    *   `App.tsx` 파일 내 토큰 유틸리티 섹션의 설명 문구를 "$PINGP is a utility token..." 으로 시작하는 문장으로 교체했습니다.

Update Token Utility description text in the UI
